summary:
The patient likely has Sturge-Weber Syndrome due to the congenital vascular plaque affecting the ophthalmic and maxillary nerves; no significant change or progression indicates its long-standing nature consistent with this condition. Key findings include facial Port-Wine stains typically associated with these neurological manifestations starting at birth. ```plaintext
Key Points Summary:

1. Congenital vascular plaque along the ophthalmic and maxillary divisions of the trigeminal nerve strongly suggests Sturge-Weber Syndrome.
2. Stable presentation since birth supports this chronic neurocutaneous condition characterized by facial Port-Wine stains and related neurological signs such as seizures.
```
